steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which choice is best supported by the sediment evidence. Two features matter: (1) fine mudstone dominates — deposited under low-flow, settling conditions, indicating a long-lived lake; (2) the mudstone shows desiccation cracks — indicating the lake periodically dried out. Coarser grain intervals indicate episodic stream input.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — a lake that occasionally experienced drying and periods with streams present: accounts for all three features — the dominant mudstone (long-lived lake), desiccation cracks (drying), and coarse intervals (stream inflow). ✓
  • Choice B — the lake never dried: directly contradicts the desiccation cracks in the mudstone. ✗
  • Choice C — the lake existed only briefly: conflicts with the mudstone’s dominance, which indicates prolonged low-energy deposition. ✗
  • Choice D — no lake existed: contradicts the fine mudstone evidence of a persistent standing water body. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
